no_changes_rationale:
Bead ddx-a946c744 — no changes this attempt.

What is done:
- Reviewed the bead description, acceptance criteria, and notes.

What is blocking:
- The bead's own <notes> field declares it has been decomposed:
  "decomposed into ddx-aee651ec (protocol package + ddx ac CLI) and
   ddx-3e60fd84 (pre_merge integration + checks_bypass + e2e).
   ddx-3e60fd84 depends on ddx-aee651ec."
- The full AC set (1-10) spans both child beads: protocol/loader/runner/ddx ac CLI
  belong to ddx-aee651ec; pre_merge integration, checks_bypass, and the
  end-to-end fixtures belong to ddx-3e60fd84.
- Implementing the parent here would duplicate or pre-empt the decomposed
  children, creating merge conflicts when those beads execute and violating the
  one-bead-one-commit boundary the queue relies on.

What a follow-up attempt would need:
- Either (a) close ddx-a946c744 administratively as superseded by its children
  (preferred — the decomposition note is the contract), or
- (b) if the parent should still be executed monolithically, remove the
  decomposition note and cancel/close the two child beads first so this attempt
  is not racing them.

Recommended action: close ddx-a946c744 as superseded; let ddx-aee651ec then
ddx-3e60fd84 execute in dependency order.
